title: The logical negation mark ¬ rendered as heavy geometric slabs. In formal logic, ¬P means 'P does not hold.' Precept makes this structural: invalid states are not refused at runtime — they are ¬declared. The definition structurally excludes them before any instance runs. The ¬ is not a warning sign but a logical fact: the invalid configuration is ¬possible by the structure of the governing contract.
conceptualBridge: The negation mark means Precept makes invalid states logically absent because they are ¬declared in the definition — ¬(invalid state exists) is guaranteed by compile-time verification, not runtime detection.
decision: Shortlisted: 4.63 avg — the ¬ slab construction achieves near-perfect scores on structural soundness, legibility, economy, and monochrome; the conceptual bridge (3.5) is the only weakness; the teal palette is fresh; the L-shaped two-slab form is maximally legible and distinctive.
